items: The previous session had repriced renewed U.S.-Iran escalation after Trump's comments.
Markets were highly sensitive to whether oil would extend or reverse its jump.
AI shares retained rebound potential if yields and crude eased.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: Oil gave back much of the prior day's surge as markets calmed.
Treasury yields eased, reducing pressure on risk assets.
AI and growth shares recovered, lifting the Nasdaq and S&P 500.
causal_narrative: This was a partial reversal of the prior day's geopolitical shock. Falling oil and yields relaxed the inflation channel, allowing growth leadership to reassert itself. The S&P 500 more than recovered the prior loss as the market treated escalation risk as less immediate.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
